author: @Alexander_Sannikov
text:
i think old-time channel subscribers subscribed to learn new stuff about things they don't know : advanced battery chemistry, supercapacitors, etc. but recently the channel shifted perspective to very simplistic projects that are often inherently and obviously flawed by design. i think a lot of people do not want to sacrifice performance of projects and use "well, it's cheap" as an excuse. the low cost can be a bonus, but performance comes first.

besides, some of your projects are genuinely good and interesting and you don't have to explain yourself for poor performance, because they work well, such as the wimshurt machine.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it's an odd one mate but there is a defection rate from the channel - people leave at a rate of around 45-50 per day but I gain around 200 or so a day - so on average the channel viewers increase by 100-150 per day - what that means in real terms is that there aren't that many 'old timers' around and it also means the reason people are here changes - to be absolutely honest people were getting tired of battery videos - after all there is only so much you can say about a battery. Flawed by design is an interesting idea as to my mind it depends what they are designed to do and that is open to interpretation - I know that as I design the projects with one thought in mind and they can often be received in a completely different way - it's a funny old world lol

replies.author: @Alexander_Sannikov
@ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: @Alexander_Sannikov I am sorry mate I have to disagree again - people watch youtube for lots of reasons - which you must know - some of the most popular videos are about babies or dancing frogs! I understand they are probably not for you - but it illustrates my point of people come to youtube for lots and lots of different reasons - I don't know if you watched the video I did called changes to the channel but it really explains what the channel is about and the wind turbine was built predominantly for those reasons - you don't have to do a thing just because it is 'optimal' you can just explore you know. Challenge a notion every now and then -I mean is connecting a resistive loads really the only way to measure the output of a wind generator?

replies.author: @Alexander_Sannikov
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering oh yeah btw you know better than anyone else that you can't measure power output of something by multiplying its open circuit voltage by short circuit current. Obviously under load you'll get less current and less voltage drop. Yet you started doing this over and over with all your generator videos. During your battery videos you at least used a proper load with proper measurements that seems to have been forsaken.

It's not about what random people find on youtube. It's about people who are your old-time subscribers getting angry because instead of getting ideas from your videos they feel deceived (like with those power measurements) so no wonder they get salty.

replies.author: @weegaz22
replies.text: As the saying goes there's many ways to skin a cat, we might all have an idea how to reach a goal in different ways, but the end result is still the same no matter how you get there, there's nothing wrong with opposing opinions, its those opposing opinions that might help you see something you have overlooked about you're approach to it, its when it descends into name calling and having an overriding superiority complex over others that's the issue.

replies.author: @malcolmwright7440
replies.text:
@weegaz22 “As the saying goes there's many ways to skin a cat, we might all have an idea how to reach a goal in different ways,”  :- 
True we are all educated different and we all have different skills

“but the end result is still the same no matter how you get there,” :-
No, the results can be different if/when the path is different, that's  why I like this channel, not only does RMS show an idea and pathway, but others give their variations, ideas and experience, not as criticism but as critique.     

“there's nothing wrong with opposing opinions, “ :-

There is, if those opinions are opposing for the sake of being argumentative, or wrong, mistaken, or from an opinionated source.

“ its those opposing opinions that might help you see something you have overlooked about you're approach to it,” :-
 
Yes but most  people will often help with helpful comments and practical suggestions.

“ its when it descends into name calling and having an overriding superiority complex over others that's the issue.” :-

Yes, and I have the perfect solution, cut them off from your social circle, problem solved.

replies.author: @weegaz22
replies.text:
@malcolmwright7440 yes results can be different if you come across something in your development process that might be more interesting to you than the initial concept you had initially started out with if you don't have a concrete goal in mind, but I had meant more from a rigidly fixed goal like say in a more commercial setting for a customer, the goal being "I must achieve XYZ to get paid", the goal and results will be roughly the same although you may have taken wildly varying ways to get to that point than someone else, 

Kind of like the development  of the TV for example, John Logie Baird did it from a mechanical standpoint and invented the first TV, but Filo Farnsworth invented it from an electronic point of view which became the more commercially viable option due to being less complicated and being much more suitable to mass production, same goal, but completely different process's.

As for the opposing opinions I do welcome them as long as like you say their respectful, as said it will either solidify the way you approach it or help you see something you may have overlooked or not considered as you may have a lack of experience or knowledge in a certain field, I didn't mean it simply from an argumentative to be argumentative point of view, more from a point of view that say a group of scientists would approach a theory, all of them may have wildly opposing theories about something until they hash it out among each other defending their theory on why ABC is more plausible than XYZ.

Although on the internet in comments sections like this it can be hard to tell the way someone is telling you something as there is a distinct lack of nuance in merely text based conversation, you don't have body language, tone, facial expression or eye contact etc etc, as you would in person to help you tell what may have simply just been an amusing and slightly sarcastic comment that could come across completely different on the way that receiving person reads into it and may feel like a personal attack.

author: @PACstove
text: Hey Robert.  Just off the top of your head, do you think that size wind gen could be enough to power a heating pad for a outdoor rescue cat?

replies.author: @charlesdickens6706
replies.text: ......you'd be talking about no more than twenty watts and even less if it's an insulated box you have for the cat with entry curtain covering the entrance. Not good idea for many reasons to supply too much heating to an outdoor animal, best to work at having a very well insulated box ( cat kennel ) . Provide only minimal heating if any . You mustn't mess up their own body's heat regulating mechanism cos that can cause death by hypothermia if the heating failed .

replies.author: @PACstove
replies.text: @charlesdickens6706 The problem is, she ducks under a neighbor's house when its cold and gets warm by some heating unit under there. Its a filthy area and i want here to stay out of there and come to a heating pad when she needs it.  The area im in funnel's the wind through the buildings so its perfect for a small unit like that.  She's a rescue cat and she an exceptional huntress who's kiilled so many mice and rats , that i lost count. Therefore, i treat her very well. I just want to ween her away from the filthy crawl space she got used to going to.

replies.author: @charlesdickens6706
replies.text: @PACstove ......good, , about the only other thing I can add and you'd also get good  advice from those who's business it is :-- is sheepskin fur on something soft to lure her to your preferred spot, sheepskin fur works and the experts might also suggest catnip sprinkled into the sheepskin .They like getting comfy in the fibreglass insulation which is not good long term cos they ingest and inhale it . In Australia cats have a curfew , they ain't permitted to be outdoor pets but folk with rodent problems do what they have to do . The ancient civilizations depended on cats to protect their stored grain .
